title: Ethiopian-Style Yaki Beef Pilaf
description: A flavorful and spicy dish combining the rich flavors of Ethiopia with the popular Japanese yaki beef. This pilaf is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the yaki beef and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Remove the beef from the skillet and set aside.
Add more oil if necessary and sauté the onion and ginger until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the pilaf spices and cook for 1 minute.
Add the rice and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add 2 cups of water to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and stir in the cooked yaki beef.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
